digest.top_line: NRG/RTOG 0848: adjuvant chemoRT adds no OS overall post-pancreatic head resection (HR 0.96, p=0.38); node-negative subgroup gains 5-yr OS 48% vs 29% (interaction p=0.022).
digest.tldr: Hepatobiliary was the day's only RT read, and it's negative: RTOG 0848's RT randomization confirms adjuvant chemoRT adds nothing over gemcitabine chemo alone after pancreatic head resection. The lone RT-relevant signal is a node-negative subgroup OS gain (5-yr 48% vs 29%), hypothesis-generating on n=91 with a gemcitabine backbone predating FOLFIRINOX-era standard.

sites.intro: A clean randomized test of adjuvant chemoRT after pancreatic head resection comes up empty overall; RT moved the needle only in the node-negative subgroup.

studies.name: NRG Oncology/RTOG 0848
studies.tldr: OS HR 0.96 (90% CI 0.79-1.18), p=0.38: adjuvant chemoRT adds no OS benefit over chemo alone after pancreatic head resection.

details.text: 🔍 RT randomization of NRG/RTOG 0848, N=354 post-resection + adjuvant gemcitabine chemo: no further tx (n=174) vs fluoropyrimidine-sensitized CXRT (n=180)

details: 📊 1° EP not met: adjuvant CXRT adds no OS benefit overall

details.text: 📐 OS HR 0.96 (90% CI 0.79-1.18), p=0.38 (1-sided); median OS 2.6 vs 2.3 yr
📊 5-yr OS by nodal status (chemo vs chemo+CXRT), interaction p=0.022
📊 Node-negative (N0) subgroup medians, chemo vs chemo+CXRT
⚠️ Node-negative OS gain is a subgroup finding (n=91, interaction p=0.022); hypothesis-generating, needs independent confirmation

details: ⚠️ RT dose, fractionation, and target volume not reported in source text

details.text: 🔗 Gemcitabine backbone (gem alone n=236, gem+erlotinib n=100) predates FOLFIRINOX-era adjuvant standard

verdict.rationale: Prospective phase III, primary OS endpoint not met (HR 0.96); reinforces omitting adjuvant CXRT. Node-negative benefit is subgroup-only (interaction p=0.022), needs independent confirmation.
verdict.audience: Resected pancreatic head adenocarcinoma, post adjuvant gemcitabine-based chemo

studies.significance: The RT read is the node-negative subgroup: 5-yr OS 28.6→48.1% and median OS 3.0→3.9 yr with added CXRT (interaction p=0.022), while node-positive pts gained nothing (21.2 vs 20.9%). Subgroup-only (n=91), but it isolates who might still warrant adjuvant CXRT once modern systemic therapy is optimized.
studies.monday_clinic: In resected pancreatic head adenocarcinoma who completed adjuvant gemcitabine-based chemo, this supports omitting fluoropyrimidine-sensitized CXRT overall and in node-positive pts; the node-negative subgroup gain is hypothesis-generating, not yet grounds to add RT.

primary_endpoint.name: Overall survival
primary_endpoint.stat_detail: 90% CI 0.79-1.18, p=0.38 (1-sided log-rank); primary OS endpoint not met

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **RT randomization** of NRG/RTOG 0848 phase 3. After curative-intent resection and adjuvant gemcitabine-based chemo without progression, **N=354** randomized to no further therapy (n=174) vs fluoropyrimidine-sensitized CXRT (n=180). **Primary: overall survival.**

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Resected **pancreatic head adenocarcinoma**; mix of N0 (n=91) and N1 (n=263), predominantly T3 (n=288 vs T1/T2 n=66), mostly negative margins (n=295). Only pts completing adjuvant chemo without progression.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Adjuvant systemic backbone gemcitabine-based: **gemcitabine alone (n=236)**, non-oxaliplatin gemcitabine combinations (n=18), gemcitabine+erlotinib (n=100). Added arm was fluoropyrimidine-sensitized CXRT.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: CXRT was **fluoropyrimidine-sensitized chemoradiotherapy** delivered after completion of adjuvant chemo. RT **dose, fractionation, and target volume not reported in source text.**

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary: overall survival.** Prespecified 5-yr OS subgroup estimates with interaction tests by nodal status, T stage, margins, CA19-9, sex, age, and systemic regimen.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Primary **OS negative: HR 0.96 (90% CI 0.79-1.18), p=0.38**; median OS 2.6 vs 2.3 yr. **Nodal-status interaction p=0.022** with a node-negative gain (see table).

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Gemcitabine backbone **predates FOLFIRINOX-era adjuvant standard**; authors note the node-negative signal needs confirmation in trials using FOLFIRINOX/FOLFOXIRI.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents resected pancreatic head adenocarcinoma completing adjuvant gemcitabine-based chemo. Does not represent unresected or borderline-resectable disease, or pts treated with FOLFIRINOX adjuvant therapy.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Node-negative OS benefit is a **subgroup finding (n=91, interaction p=0.022)**, hypothesis-generating. Dated gemcitabine backbone; **RT technique not reported** in source.

open_questions: Does adjuvant CXRT benefit node-negative resected pancreatic cancer?
Confirmation with modern FOLFIRINOX adjuvant backbone

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Chemoradiotherapy Versus Chemotherapy for Pancreatic Cancer (ADJUPANC)
related_trials.brief_summary: In this trial, we aim to compare the outcomes of adjuvant chemoradiotherapy with chemotherapy for patients with resected pancreatic cancer who are at high risk of disease progressions.

related_trials.brief_title: Radiotherapy With GX Regimen as Adjuvant Therapy for High-risk Patients Following Pancreatic Cancer Surgery
related_trials.brief_summary: Pancreatic cancer is a highly fatal malignant tumor. Simple surgical operations can no longer meet the treatment needs of pancreatic cancer patients. Postoperative adjuvant chemotherapy has a significant effect, which can effectively prevent or delay tumor recurrence and prolong the overall survival period of pancreatic cancer patients. related_trials.brief_title: Testing the Use of the Usual Chemotherapy Before and After Surgery for Removable Pancreatic Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase III trial compares perioperative chemotherapy (given before and after surgery) versus adjuvant chemotherapy (given after surgery) for the treatment of pancreatic cancer that can be removed by surgery (removable/resectable). Chemotherapy drugs, such as fluorouracil, irinotecan, leucovorin, and oxaliplatin, work in different ways to stop the growth of tumor cells, either by killing the cells, by stopping them from dividing, or by stopping them from spreading.
